span.p:
For each output-bearing function
(sales, CS, services, support)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Take the TARGET
(bookings / accounts to serve / backlog)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Compute STEADY-STATE productivity per head
from HISTORY (not quota/aspiration)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Required ramped capacity <= what the
hiring plan delivers in time?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
NOT FEASIBLE. Choose: (a) pull hiring forward
(more cost sooner), (b) raise the productivity
assumption (justify it or it's a hockey stick),
(c) lower the target. Don't assert capacity
the org can't field
